-module(solve_mod).
-export([solve/2]).

%% solve(Nums, K)
%% Finds the maximum score of a valid alternating subsequence with distance constraint K.
solve(Nums, K) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    if N == 0 ->
        0;
    true ->
        % dp[i][0]: Max score ending at i, where the sequence must continue by going UP (i.e., nums[i] was a valley or start). (Pattern ... > nums[i] < ...)
        % dp[i][1]: Max score ending at i, where the sequence must continue by going DOWN (i.e., nums[i] was a peak or start). (Pattern ... < nums[i] > ...)
        
        % Initialize DP table: list of size N, where each element is [dp_valley, dp_peak]
        DP = lists:replicate(N, [0, 0]),
        
        MaxScore = 0,
        
        % Fold over indices i from 0 to N-1
        MaxScore = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
            Nums_I = element(I, Nums),
            
            % Base case: subsequence of length 1
            % A single element can start a sequence that needs to go up (Valley start) or down (Peak start).
            % Since it's length 1, the score is just Nums_I.
            % We initialize both states to Nums_I, as they are the maximum score achievable ending at I with that required continuation pattern.
            DP_I = [Nums_I, Nums_I],
            
            % Iterate over all possible previous indices j < i
            {NewDP_I, Max_I} = lists:foldl(fun({CurrentDP_I, Current_Max}, J) ->
                % Check distance constraint
                if I - J >= K ->
                    Nums_J = element(J, Nums),
                    % Retrieve previous DP values for J
                    DP_J = element(J, DP),
                    DP_J_Valley = element(0, DP_J), % dp[j][0]
                    DP_J_Peak = element(1, DP_J),   % dp[j][1]

                    % Calculate potential new values for DP_I based on transitions from J
                    
                    % Case 1: New element Nums_I becomes a Valley (dp[i][0]). 
                    % Previous must be a Peak (dp[j][1]), and Nums_J > Nums_I (Decreasing transition: ... > Nums_I)
                    New_Val_Valley = 
                        if Nums_J > Nums_I ->
                            max(element(0, CurrentDP_I), DP_J_Peak + Nums_I)
                        ;
                            element(0, CurrentDP_I)
                        end,
                    
                    % Case 2: New element Nums_I becomes a Peak (dp[i][1]). 
                    % Previous must be a Valley (dp[j][0]), and Nums_J < Nums_I (Increasing transition: ... < Nums_I)
                    New_Val_Peak = 
                        if Nums_J < Nums_I ->
                            max(element(1, CurrentDP_I), DP_J_Valley + Nums_I)
                        ;
                            element(1, CurrentDP_I)
                        end,
                    
                    NewDP_I = [New_Val_Valley, New_Val_Peak],
                    New_Max_I = max(Current_Max, New_Val_Valley, New_Val_Peak),
                    
                    {NewDP_I, New_Max_I}
                else
                    {CurrentDP_I, Current_Max} % Distance constraint failed
                end
            end, {DP_I, Nums_I}, 0..I-1),
            
            % Update DP table with the final calculated values for I
            DP_New = lists:replace(DP, I, NewDP_I),
            
            % Update overall max score
            Max_Score_I = max(MaxScore, element(0, NewDP_I), element(1, NewDP_I)),
            
            {DP_New, Max_Score_I}

        end, {DP, MaxScore}, 0..N-1)

        MaxScore
    end.

-module(solve_module).

solve(Nums, K) ->
    case N of
        0 -> 0;
        _ ->
            do_solve(Nums, K, N)
    end.

do_solve(Nums, K, N) ->
    % dp[i][0]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] is a valley (or start, expecting next element > nums[i]).
    % dp[i][1]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] is a peak (or start, expecting next element < nums[i]).
    
    % Initialize DP table: List of [Valley_Score, Peak_Score] for each index.
    % Initial score for any single element subsequence is the element's value itself.
    
    Initialize_DP(N, Nums).

Initialize_DP(N, Nums) ->
    % Initialize dp table: List of length N, where each element is a tuple {v_score, p_score}
    DP = lists:nth(0, N, fun(I) -> {nums:nth(I, Nums), nums:nth(I, Nums)} end),
    
    Max_Score = 0,

    fun(I) ->
        I_val = nums:nth(I, Nums),
        
        % Current DP values for index I initialized to length 1 subsequence
        Current_DP = {I_val, I_val},
        
        % Iterate over previous indices J < I
        New_DP = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, J) ->
            case lists:nth(J, DP) of
                {J_v, J_p} ->
                    J_val = nums:nth(J, Nums),
                    
                    % Check distance constraint
                    if I - J >= K ->
                        % Case 1: nums[i] is a valley (New_V). Previous must be a peak (J_p). Transition: J_val > I_val
                        New_V = if J_val > I_val -> max(fst(Acc), J_p + I_val) ; fst(Acc) end,
                        
                        % Case 2: nums[i] is a peak (New_P). Previous must be a valley (J_v). Transition: J_val < I_val
                        New_P = if J_val < I_val -> max(snd(Acc), J_v + I_val) ; snd(Acc) end,
                        
                        {New_V, New_P}
                    else
                        Acc
                    end
        end, Current_DP, lists:seq(0, I-1)),
        
        % Update DP table and max score
        DP_new = lists:append(DP, [New_DP]),
        Max_Score_new = max(Max_Score, max(fst(New_DP), snd(New_DP))),
        
        {DP_new, Max_Score_new}
    end(0),

    % The fold/recursion structure in Erlang is complex for simulating iterative DP.
    % We must use a recursive helper function that accumulates the DP table and MaxScore.
    recursive_dp(0, [], 0)

recursive_dp(I, DP_List, Max_Score) when I == N ->
    Max_Score;

recursive_dp(I, DP_List, Max_Score) ->
    I_val = lists:nth(I, Nums),
    
    % Base case: Length 1 subsequence
    Current_V = I_val,
    Current_P = I_val,
    
    % Iterate over previous indices J < I
    {Final_V, Final_P} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, J) ->
        % Acc is {Current_V, Current_P}
        
        % Get DP state for J
        J_State = lists:nth(J, DP_List),
        {J_v, J_p} = J_State,
        J_val = lists:nth(J, Nums),
        
        % Check distance constraint
        if I - J >= K ->
            % Case 1: nums[i] is a valley (New_V). Previous must be a peak (J_p). Transition: J_val > I_val
            New_V = if J_val > I_val -> max(fst(Acc), J_p + I_val) ; fst(Acc) end,
            
            % Case 2: nums[i] is a peak (New_P). Previous must be a valley (J_v). Transition: J_val < I_val
            New_P = if J_val < I_val -> max(snd(Acc), J_v + I_val) ; snd(Acc) end,
            
            {New_V, New_P}
        else
            Acc
        end
    end, {Current_V, Current_P}, lists:seq(0, I-1)),

    % Update Max Score
    New_Max_Score = max(Max_Score, max(Final_V, Final_P)),
    
    % Recurse
    recursive_dp(I + 1, lists:append(DP_List, [{Final_V, Final_P}]), New_Max_Score).
